title: RiboGreen Quantification using a qPCR machine
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"isiuu","text":"This protocol describes how to use a qPCR machine to perform RiboGreen quantification of the concentration of extracellular RNA in a sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"rxi8b","text":"Extracellular RNAs (exRNAs) have been identified in every biofluid that has been tested. They have been found in extracellular vesicles, ribonucleoprotein complexes and lipoprotein complexes. exRNAs are interesting because they may serve as signalling molecules between cells, they have the potential to serve as biomarkers for prediction and diagnosis of disease, and exRNAs or the extracellular particles that carry them might be used for therapeutic purposes.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pxtqc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hyb3c","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ylapm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jxjjb","text":"The Sample and Assay Standards Working Group of the Extracellular RNA Communication Consortium (ERCC) is a group of laboratories funded by the U.S. National Institutes of Health to develop robust and standardized methods for collecting and processing of biofluids, separating different types of exRNA-containing particles and isolating and analyzing exRNAs. In our first joint endeavour, we held a series of conference calls and in-person meetings to survey the methods used among our members, placed them in the context of the current literature and used our findings to identify areas in which the identification of robust methodologies would promote rapid advancements in the exRNA field.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uqych","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"47atr","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ufm6i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qhios","text":"A full list of the protocols developed during this effort is available at the exRNA Portal, the ERCC's website ( \"http://exrna.org/resources/protocols/\":http://exrna.org/resources/protocols/ ). This protocol for performing RiboGreen quantification is one of the final steps in the process, allowing quantification of the amount of extracellular RNA found in a sample or biofluid.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"tkgyw","text":"Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jh6sp","text":"Quant-It RiboGreen Assay Kit* (Life Technologies, catalog # R11490)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"w5gmj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vehz","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65kn9","text":"qPCR machine\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e0iae","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"amrzj","text":"96-well plates compatible with qPCR machine","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute 20X TE (Component B) 1:20 in RNase-free water to make 1X TE Buffer.
Make standards in microfuge tubes using the 100 μg/mL rRNA standard included in the Quant-It RiboGreen Assay Kit. These can be stored for future use. See figure in Figures section.

Based on the expected concentrations of the samples, make the appropriate dilution of the RiboGreen reagent (Component A) in 1X TE (1:400 for samples between 2-100 ng/μL, 1:4,000 for samples between 0.05-1 ng/μL). You will need 20 μL of the diluted reagent for each sample and each standard.
Based on the expected concentrations of the samples, set up the assay plate with 1 μL each of the appropriate standards.
In each well, mix 1 μL of sample/standard with 19 μL of the appropriate dilution of the RiboGreen reagent. Pipet up and down to mix.

Read in a qPCR machine (use FAM/SYBR channel). Export raw fluorescence values. Draw the standard curve, and use the linear trend line to generate the formula to calculate the concentration for each sample.

Please note that you must use the Quant-It RiboGreen Assay Kit, as listed above, NOT* the Quant-It RNA Assay, Broad Range, as the Broad Range kit uses a different dye in the far-red range which cannot be detected by a qPCR machine.
The authors declare no competing financial interests.
